title: Creamy Malaysian Tofu Curry
description: A tangy, creamy, halal-friendly tofu curry packed with szechuan peppercorn flavor, perfect for a cozy date night.
instructions: 1. Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
3. Stir in the minced garlic, grated ginger, crushed szechuan peppercorns, and tomato paste; cook for 1‑2 minutes until fragrant.
4. Deglaze the pan with a splash of coconut milk, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
5. Add the remaining coconut milk, tamarind paste, palm sugar, soy sauce, and lime juice; bring to a gentle simmer.
6. Gently fold in the cubed tofu, ensuring it is coated in the sauce.
7. Cover and let the curry simmer for 8‑10 minutes, allowing the tofu to absorb the flavors.
8. Taste and adjust seasoning—add more lime juice for acidity or more palm sugar for sweetness if needed.
9. Remove from heat and let sit for 2 minutes to thicken slightly.
10. Garnish with freshly chopped cilantro before serving.
11. Serve hot over steamed jasmine rice or with warm flatbread.
